sanskrit: मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय । मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव ॥
hindiTranslation: हे धनञ्जय  मेरे बढ़कर इस जगत् का दूसरा कोई किञ्चिन्मात्र भी कारण नहीं है ।  जैसे सूतकी मणियाँ सूतके धागेमें पिरोयी हुई होती हैं, ऐसे ही यह सम्पूर्ण जगत् मेरेमें ही ओतप्रोत है ॥
englishTranslation: O Dhananjaya, nothing exists higher than Me. All this world is strung on Me like pearls on a thread.
essence: Everything is held in one sustaining reality.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says nothing is greater than him, and the whole world is held together within him like beads on a string.
simpleInsight: You can stop feeling cut off from life. What holds everything also holds you.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen and overwhelmed. Krishna has already explained the two levels of nature and now reveals the deepest claim: nothing is beyond him, and the entire world is woven through him. This shifts Arjuna from fear to a larger vision of reality.
modernRelevance: You are staring at a hospital corridor, waiting for news that could change everything. The room feels isolated, but this verse says nothing is truly separate from the ground that holds it all.
translationLiteral: मत्तः (than Me) / परतरं (higher) / न (not) / अन्यत् (other) / किञ्चिद् (anything) / अस्ति (exists) / धनञ्जय (O Dhananjaya) / मयि (in Me) / सर्वम् (all) / इदम् (this) / प्रोतम् (strung) / सूत्रे (on a thread) / मणिगणाः (clusters of gems) / इव (like)
